entry.title: Re: My Inbox - Manage My Substitution Report
entry.content:
Hello,


There is no classic report.
Only you can see your substitutions in My Inbox, you can also create them via the Fiori app "Manage my substitution rules".
If you are at Admin level, you can use the Fiori app "Manage Teams and Responsibilities" to set up and create substitutes. You will then need the corresponding admin authorization role.

Best regards

Dennis

entry.title: Boost productivity and engagement with the 1H 2025 SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone release
entry.content:
At SAP, we continue to transform how organizations connect people, processes, and information. In the 1H 2025 release, we are excited to introduce key innovations in SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone—including Home Page Quick Action Cards, Supporting Alumni Experience, and the new Payroll Landing Page. These updates further empower employees, managers, alumni, and administrators with a more intelligent, connected, and efficient digital workplace. 

Home Page Quick Action Cards: Take Action Instantly 

Work happens fast—and so should the tools that support it. With Home Page Quick Action Cards, users can complete tasks such as requesting time off, clocking time, viewing their learning, managing goals, accessing their pay statements directly from their customized workspace, without navigating through multiple menus or applications. Deep links connect directly to key pages like the People Profile, Org Chart, Time Sheet, and Learning Home, while snack UIs enable quick in-place interactions 

Key benefits: 

  • Seamless action within the workspace: Employees can approve requests, update data, and complete tasks instantly—right from the homepage. 
  • Effortless navigation to connected systems: Deep links streamline access to other platforms for cross-functional workflows. 
  • Prioritized, actionable insights: Users see urgent tasks first, enabling smarter, faster decisions. 
  • Simplified task management: Employees manage time, team, and personal data efficiently in one experience. 

Supporting Alumni Experience: Foster Long-Term Connections 

Employee relationships do not end at offboarding. The new Supporting Alumni Experience feature empowers organizations to maintain meaningful, mutually beneficial connections with former employees. 

Key benefits: 

  • Empower alumni with access and transparency: Alumni retain access to their personal data, ensuring transparency and control even after they leave the organization. 
  • Enable self-service for alumni: Reduce HR workload by allowing alumni to update contact details, access pay slips, or retrieve employment documents on their own. 
  • Enhance talent pools and referral networks: Support ongoing alumni engagement, creating opportunities for future collaborations, rehires, or referrals. 

Payroll Landing Page: Streamline Payroll Administration 

Managing payroll requires quick, consolidated access to information. The new Payroll Landing Page brings together everything payroll administrators need for their daily work—right in one place within SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. A beta program is currently in progress, and customers interested in early access are encouraged to contact their SAP account manager to sign up. 

Key benefits: 

  • Centralized access to reporting, tickets, and resources: Administrators can easily navigate payroll reporting, support tickets, and key internal documents from a single workspace. 
  • Flexible, extendable platform: The landing page provides baseline payroll content, including UI integration cards and APIs, empowering customers to extend and build a customized payroll experience tailored to their needs. 
  • Initial focus on Employee Central Payroll: The landing page is optimized for ECP today, with support for SAP SuccessFactors Payroll planned in the future. 

Building a Connected Workplace, From Hire to Alumni and Beyond 

With these innovations, SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone continues to evolve as the one stop digital hub for employees, managers, alumni, and administrators—empowering every role to act, manage, and collaborate with greater ease and efficiency. 

For a more comprehensive look at all our innovations, please visit What’s New Viewer on the SAP Help Portal. For more solution info, please visit the SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone website.

entry.title: Re: Turn on Tax Number Categories
entry.content:
Hello @BárbaraJorquera ,

Thank you for your feedback.

The Tax Number Category Duplication Check is handled through SSCUI 102415 – "Define Checking Rules for Tax Numbers." If this SSCUI seems blocked, it could be due to a lack of authorization or scope item activation. Please confirm that scope item 1Y2 (Business Partner – General Data Integration) is active and that your role has the required business catalog access.

Let me know if you need further information

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: The workflow delegation is not shown in workflow log...
entry.content:
Hello @Pramanan 

In the Fiori My Inbox app, workflow delegation is apparent, but it does not appear in classic workflow logs (SWI1, SWI5, SBWP) or standard workflow tracing tools. This is anticipated behaviour since workflow logs monitor the original approver rather than the delegated user. To gain visibility in these logs, a custom enhancement or extension leveraging BAdIs, such as SWF_WORKFLOW_AGENTS, might be necessary logs.

SAP Note 2526184 – Workflow Delegation Visibility in My Inbox

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Fiori Elements: @UI.CreateHidden with $Not + $Path expression is not working
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm currently facing an issue with a Fiori Elements List Report application based on CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model). I’m trying to conditionally hide the “Create” button using the `@UI.CreateHidden` annotation with a $Not + $Path expression, like this:

annotate CatalogService.Books with @(
  UI.CreateHidden : { $edmJson: { $Not: { $Path: '/CatalogService.EntityContainer/FeatureControl/createEnabled' } } }
);

The EDMX output looks correct:

<Annotation Term="UI.CreateHidden">
  <Not>
    <Path>/CatalogService.EntityContainer/FeatureControl/createEnabled</Path>
  </Not>
</Annotation>

The singleton entity FeatureControl is correctly exposed and returns the following response:

{
  "createEnabled": false,
  "createHidden": true
}

When I reference createHidden directly with a simple $Path, it works as expected and the Create button is hidden. However, when using the $Not + $Path version, the Create button remains visible.

Has anyone successfully used $Not expressions in annotations like `@UI.CreateHidden` in a Fiori Elements List Report?

Any insights or workarounds would be greatly appreciated.

UI5 version: 1.135.0

Git repository link: https://github.com/miyasuta/cap-feature-control

entry.title: Filter Variable on Fields with Compound Keys
entry.content:
Hello Expert.

Let us assume to have an Analytical Model in Sap Datasphere and to want to create a Filter Variable on a Field of the Analytical Model, where the Field has Compound Keys. My question is: how does the Filter Variable behave with respect to the Compound keys? As far as I see, it looses the compouding fields and all the related information (such as texts); besides, if it looses the compounding fields, on a matter of fact, I don't know how it applies the filter in the Analytical Model.

entry.title: Showing Onboarding Documents in Employee Central Profile
entry.content:
Before You Start

For reflecting onboarding documents in new hire employee profile after job start date, on the first step you must configure standard creating custom data collection object procedure as defined in SAP Help Portal. You can see steps below link.

Link: Configuring Onboarding Data Collection with Default Data Collection Object Instance

Note: Please do not forget association between custom onboarding document collection and Onboarding data collection user configuration as you know userConfig and cust_subjectUser configuration. It ensures the relations among uploaded documents and new hire profile. Otherwise this configuration does not work in your system.

Creating A Custom MDF for EC Profile

After standard onboarding data collection configuration, you can start to create a custom MDF for EC profile.

• Set externalCode as User. In detail, visibility must be editable.
• MDF API visibility must be editable.

• Add association under of new object. Choose onbaording data collection MDF and set Type Valid When.

• Set visibility Editable. 
• Write cust_subjectUser to Destination Object Column
• Write externalCode to Source Object Column

After this process, you can configure an UI in Manage Configuration UI page.

After UI configuration, you must create a new portlet in Configure People Profile and add new custom MDF object in this section.

Set Up Integration Between Two Objects

• Go to Integration Centre page and create a new integration as below.

• Chose option as below.

• Choose custom onboarding document collection object as source.
• In Configuration Fields page, switch field mapping view at the right corner.
• Add custom EC profile object as destination object.

• Drag and drop subjectUser of source entity to externalCode of destination entity.
• Drag and drop externalCode of source entity to externalCode inside of association of destination entity association.
• Add modify since filter and set last run time in Filter and Sort page.

Schedule and Save your new integration.

Now you can see onboarding documents in new employees profile after they started to work. According to permissions, employees can access this portlet and download onboarding documents.

Click documents icon and see onboarding documents.

If you see more details in documents sheet, you can restrict to access about custom onboarding object in permission role as below.

Or you can change labels of your custom onboarding document object, such as cust_subjectUser field label can be User.

entry.title: COPC-ETO-Scenario - Results Analysis: (07) POC Method Using Progress Version (132)
entry.content:
In this document Am going to share about functionality of Product Costing ETO-Scenario - Results Analysis – POC method using Progress Version core configuration and Earned Value calculation Results. 

1. Illustration introduction
2. Customizing details
3. User Activities
4. Report

ETO-Scenario - Results Analysis: (07) POC Method Using Progress Version (132)

Use 

You can use the method POC Method on Basis of Progress Version for projects.

This results analysis method calculates the percentage of completion (POC) on the basis of the project’s progress version.

 The POC method is often used in Britain and North America. Since German law does not allow unrealized profits to be reported, the POC method is used in Germany only for internal information purposes.

1. Customizing details

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Status: REL

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Status: TECO

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Status: FNBL

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Just would like to show you the Project Structure CJ20N & WBS Hierarchy

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

WBS Hierarchy flow:

 

 

 

 

Illustration Example:

 You have planned revenue of EUR 200,000 and costs of EUR 120,000 for a sales order. The planned order quantity is 100 units. The base quantity is the invoiced quantity.

 Period 04:

 In period 04, you have actual costs of EUR 20,000. You have not billed the customer.

 In results analysis, the system calculates the following data:

  • No costs affecting net income
  • No revenue affecting net income
  • Capitalized costs using the formula: Capitalized Costs = Actual Costs – Cost of Sales (= costs affecting net income)
  • You then settle the capitalized costs to FI and EC-PCA

Note: Just for quick reference about the calculated values in the MS Excel.

 

 

 

 

 

At month-end, the following transactions need to be executed in sequence in order to calculate the Recognized Revenue and Unearned Revenue

      1- CNE1 – Calculating the Project Progress

This step measures the project progress based measurement method defined in the WBS element. The project progress result is stored in the progress analysis version “132”.

    2- KKA2 – Calculating the Project Revenue and Cost

This step valuates the WBS element according to the setting of Results Analysis

T-Code: KKA2

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Period: 5

In period 05 actual costs increase to EUR 80,000. You deliver to your customer and send him a milestone invoice for EUR 100,000. The invoiced quantity is 40 units. The order is partially delivered and partially billed.

 In results analysis, the system calculates the following data:

  • Revenue affecting net income of EUR 80,000
  • Costs affecting net income of EUR 48,000
  • Capitalized costs of EUR 32,000
  • Surplus revenue of EUR 20,000

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

T-Code: KKA2

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Progress Analysis in Detail: Earned Value Calculation result

 

 

 

 

 

Progress Analysis: Earned Value Report

 

 

 

Period 06

 In period 06 actual costs increase to USD 90,000. You deliver a second amount to your customer and send him a second milestone billing for USD 90,000. Total revenue is USD 190,000. The total invoiced quantity is 80 units. The order is partially delivered and partially billed. In results analysis, the system calculates the following data:

  • Costs affecting net income of USD 96,000
  • Revenue affecting net income of USD 160,000
  • Reserves for unrealized costs of USD 6,000
  • Surplus revenue of USD 30,000  

No capitalized costs. The capitalized costs calculated in the prior period are canceled in period 05.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

T-Code: KKA2

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

7th Period

In period 07 actual costs increase to USD 130,000. You deliver the remaining goods and send the customer the final invoice for USD 10,000. Total revenue is USD 200,000. The total invoiced quantity is 100 units. The order is now fully delivered and fully invoiced. In results analysis, the system calculates the following data: Costs affecting net income of USD 130,000 Revenue affecting net income of USD 200,000

 

 

 

 

 

The income statement shows the following values:

 

 

 

 

T-Code: KKA2

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Progress Analysis Report: Earned Value Details.

 

 

 

Conclusion: Your comments are welcomed to improvise this blog.

entry.title: Re: Actions in Behavior Definition For CDS Custom Entity
entry.content:
what I think is that you use READ ENTITIES OF zc_exchange_bank IN LOCAL MODE, you're attempting to read from the RAP transactional buffer. However for custom entities that get data from external sources this buffer is initially empty. The data you see in your Fiori UI comes directly from your query implementation class but those instances never get automatically stored in the transactional buffer unless explicit CUD operations are performed on them.
I think you need to bypass the buffer reading attempt and work directly with the key values that are already provided to your method. use these keys (ForexType, SapDate, CurrencyCode) directly to perform your Z-table insert, then populate the RESULT parameter to update the UI with new status values.

METHOD SetExchange.
  
  " Data structures for Z-table 
  DATA: ztable_data TYPE TABLE OF ztable_exchange_log,
        failed      TYPE RESPONSE FOR FAILED zc_exchange_bank,
        reported    TYPE RESPONSE FOR REPORTED zc_exchange_bank.
  
  " Key structure matching entity primary keys
  TYPES: BEGIN OF ts_entity_key,
           ForexType    TYPE c LENGTH 1,
           SapDate      TYPE d,
           CurrencyCode TYPE c LENGTH 5,
         END OF ts_entity_key.
  
  " Tables for fetching process
  DATA: keys_for_fetch TYPE TABLE OF ts_entity_key,
        full_data      TYPE TABLE OF zc_exchange_bank_type.
  
  " Extract keys from selected entries
  LOOP AT keys ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<key>).
    APPEND VALUE #( 
      ForexType    = <key>-%key-ForexType
      SapDate      = <key>-%key-SapDate
      CurrencyCode = <key>-%key-CurrencyCode
    ) TO keys_for_fetch.
  ENDLOOP.
  
  " Fetch complete data from web service via query implementation
  IF keys_for_fetch IS NOT INITIAL.
    TRY.
        DATA(query_impl) = cl_abap_get_instance=>get_instance( 'ZCL_RAP_EXCHANGE' ).
        
        CALL METHOD query_impl->('GET_DATA_BY_KEYS')
          EXPORTING
            it_keys    = keys_for_fetch
          RECEIVING
            rt_result  = full_data.
            
      CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(error).
        " Handle fetch errors
        LOOP AT keys ASSIGNING <key>.
          INSERT VALUE #( %tky = <key>-%tky ) INTO TABLE failed-exchange.
          INSERT VALUE #( %tky = <key>-%tky
                          %msg = NEW zcx_rap_error( 
                                   severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
                                   textid   = 'FETCH_ERROR'
                                   text     = '' ) 
                        ) INTO TABLE reported-exchange.
        ENDLOOP.
        failed   = failed.
        reported = reported.
        RETURN.
    ENDTRY.
  ENDIF.
  
  " Process data and prepare Z-table records and UI updates
  LOOP AT keys ASSIGNING <key>.
    READ TABLE full_data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<entity>) 
      WITH KEY ForexType    = <key>-%key-ForexType
               SapDate      = <key>-%key-SapDate
               CurrencyCode = <key>-%key-CurrencyCode.
               
    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      " Prepare Z-table entry with key and non-key fields
      APPEND VALUE #(
        forex_type    = <key>-%key-ForexType
        sap_date      = <key>-%key-SapDate
        currency_code = <key>-%key-CurrencyCode
        isim          = <entity>-Isim          
        sapdate_ui    = <entity>-SapDate_UI    
        processed_on  = sy-datum
        processed_by  = sy-uname
        status        = 'COMPLETED'
      ) TO ztable_data.
      
      " Prepare UI update data
      APPEND VALUE #(
        %tky  = <key>-%tky
        %data = VALUE #( 
                  StatusCode = '1'                 
                  StatusIcon = 'sap-icon://accept'  
                )
      ) TO result.
    ELSE
      " Handle missing data
      INSERT VALUE #( %tky = <key>-%tky ) INTO TABLE failed-exchange.
      INSERT VALUE #( %tky = <key>-%tky
                      %msg = NEW zcx_rap_error( 
                               severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
                               textid   = 'NOT_FOUND' 
                               text     = 'Entity data not found' )
                    ) INTO TABLE reported-exchange.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.
  
  " Save data to Z-table
  IF ztable_data IS NOT INITIAL.
    TRY.
        INSERT ztable_exchange_log FROM TABLE @ztable_data.
        IF sy-subrc <> 0.
          " Handle database insert errors
          CLEAR result.
          LOOP AT keys ASSIGNING <key>.
            INSERT VALUE #( %tky = <key>-%tky ) INTO TABLE failed-exchange.
            INSERT VALUE #( %tky = <key>-%tky
                            %msg = NEW zcx_rap_error( 
                                     severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
                                     textid   = 'DB_ERROR'
                                     text     = 'Failed to save to database' )
                          ) INTO TABLE reported-exchange.
          ENDLOOP.
        ENDIF.
      CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(db_error).
        " Handle database exceptions
        CLEAR result.
        LOOP AT keys ASSIGNING <key>.
          INSERT VALUE #( %tky = <key>-%tky ) INTO TABLE failed-exchange.
          INSERT VALUE #( %tky = <key>-%tky
                          %msg = NEW zcx_rap_error( 
                                   severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
                                   textid   = 'EXCEPTION'
                                   text     = '' )
                        ) INTO TABLE reported-exchange.
        ENDLOOP.
    ENDTRY.
  ENDIF.

  " Set framework response parameters
  failed   = failed.
  reported = reported.
ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: Re: WFA Starter Packages Not Loading with No import logs...
entry.content:
Hi @sfsapsvsv - When you ran the initial load, have you upload the template configuration successfully? If you run initial load before uploading the template configuration, the error is most likely because there is no WFAoH data factory configuration there.

The support has tried to upload the standard template configuration to the instance and found no issue. Please reach out to support if you want more detail.

Also, when extracting the template configuration, please use 7-zip to unzip the file. A different partner before experienced error when he used the standard "Extract All" option on Windows 10 File Explorer.

entry.title: Next Generation - IT Operating Model (SAP Centric)
entry.content:
This blog aims to define the future Information Technology Operating Model, focusing on governance, agility and value.  It does not focus on SAP RISE, Clean Core or AI, although they will play a major role in your future Enterprise IT Operating Model (especially AIAgent).  "Who is the blog for?" Everyone who is involved in the lifecycle of SAP Systems and ancillary technologies, as it outlines the approach to Solution Architecture, along with online resources, methodologies, and learning assets, to define and reinforce what your future Target Operating Model should look like.  Your operating model and IT capability need to transition from primarily  development, security and operations toward Analytics, Data products, Integration and Development.  Which means you and your colleagues also need to shift their focus, reusing existing skills, and gaining new ones, so that everyone is empowered with the ability to add value.

"Why?"  Because owning, securing and operating your own systems is both sunk and prospective costs.  Creating value by productising your own data, putting it to work with analytical applications and AI, and extending processes to suit your business needs, all have a direct impact on net earnings.  "So what? This is not new or noteworthy, it's just common sense, there are however, entry level and transition barriers, for instance, does this mean all employees need to be data scientists and developers?"  Well, yes and no.  SAP has gone to great lengths to lower the skill entry level threshold, with platforms and tools designed with affordance and AI features, such as the SAP Build Family which embodies Low Code / No Code (LCNC) concepts, On-Stack with S/4HANA with Key User Extensibility, Side-by-side with SAP Business Technology Platform, and much more, so that your workforce can intuitively discover and realise the digital experience that best works for them.  Which brings us back to the aim of this blog 'to set out the steps to adapt your Information Technology Operating Model.'

There's some big statements in the first two paragraphs, so let's recap and dig a little deeper.

  • Analytics Data Integration and Development Value:
    • Organisations need to adapt their DevSecOps IT Operating Model to align with adding business value.  
  • The IT Operating Model is a continually evolving target:
    • Adoption
    • Integration
    • Compliance
    • Continuous innovation
    • Workforce enablement
    • A core principle and strategic objective of, future proof IT

Synopsis

Organizations have, and continue, to invest heavily in their digital workforce.  The operations team: network, operating system, database administrators, the development team: frontend, integration and backend development, the security and compliance teams, project managers, architects, and the various application and data support teams, the list goes on.  These teams are instrumental in keeping the company running, however generational knowledge is often lost, even if it's been carefully curated and catalogued.

As organisations transition to the cloud, it is tempting to maintain the status-quo, due in part, to difficulty in change management or the unquantified risk, such as having to 'reinvent the wheel'.  This leads organisations adopting a 'lift and shift' approach, with the assumption that, change will somehow be minimised.  This approach can be achieved using existing skills but it adds little to no value, and the software architecture is already constrained by the sunset of various products.

In adopting SAP RISE, a large proportion of the application stack transitions from your organisations IT teams to SAP and its Partners, this does not mean that your organisation will not need an operations team, it does mean that they will have to refocus toward service management, monitoring, and governance, risk and compliance.  Development teams will have less technical freedom with an increase in Extension Technology per Extension Task, therefore the need for 'Pro Code' developers will reduce, whilst the 'Key Users Extensibility' (formerly 'Citizen Developer') will increase.  This will require strong development governance (see previous point).  The skills threshold for data and analytics has also reduced, comprising of four distinct entities each with their own features and capabilities.  Your organisation needs to productise data so that it can be consumed by the analytics platform of your choice, and SAP AI capabilities.  More than 70% of integration projects fail, so organisations need all the help they can get.

SAP has recognised that strong integration governance is instrumental in constraining complexity and cost, responding with a robust template based Integration Solution Advisory Methodology to reduce "tight coupling between business applications and increasing resilience."  Security and compliance will shift, from an organisation being solely responsible, to a shared responsibility model.  This will require auditing, and your organisation will have to manage and facilitate this.

Enterprise Architecture

A foreword on Enterprise Architecture (EA).  I see EA in terms of a recipe book, with some being are better than others, but it's all a matter of taste, right?  TOGAF, ZACHMANN, FEAF, Gartner and SAP EA Methodology, each have unique strengths and approaches for aligning IT with the business, for the remainder of this blog, and for simplification of context I'll compare only to the SAP EA and TOGAF (interchangeably) as they are closest in alignment.  The operating model is an interpretation of one or more recipes to produce a culinary experience.  I'm not suggesting that every experience is good, so it's worthwhile starting with something that’s 'proven' (continuing the culinary metaphor).  In other words The IT Operating Model sets out the practical steps to execute the Enterprise Architecture (vision).  Enterprise Architecture has three core concerns which are addressed in 9 common use cases:

  • Promote Growth
    • Monoliths to microservices, cloud transformation, IoT Architecture
  • Reduce complexity
    • Post merger integration, application rationalisation, integration architecture
  • Ensure compliance
    • Technology risk management, data compliance, governance standards

"So, how do the core concerns of EA translate to the IT Operating Model?"

The IT Operating Model

Gartner defines the outcomes for the IT Operating Model (ITOM) as:

  1. Enable IT efficiency. For example, run projects and efficiently keep the business running.
  2. Enhance business performance, such as improved external customer experience, improved user productivity or cost optimization through business process automation.
  3. Transform the business by creating enterprise wide competitive advantage or growth.

IT Operating Model: How CIOs Show Its Value | Gartner

Mapping EA to ITOM

Well it's not a 1:1 mapping, in fact I would be doing it a disservice to even try, because IT Operating Models are fundamentally about people, whereas EA focuses predominantly on things, or in TOGAF terms ' Requirements Management'.  EA is a top down approach from strategic objectives through to infrastructure and network architecture, and ITOMs are commonly viewed as pyramids or as opposing forces of; cost, quality and flexibility (but you can only really favour two).  Operations, security and development are cross topics touching each of the 8 layers of Enterprise Architecture:

  1. Business Capabilities - Business Architecture
  2. Business Processes - Processes Architecture
  3. Functional Capabilities - Functional Architecture
  4. Application & Information - Application & Information Architecture
  5. Software Components - Software & Service Architecture
  6. Integration Platforms - Integration Architecture
  7. Data - Physical Data Architecture
  8. Infrastructure Cloud - Infrastructure and Network Architecture

IT Architecture, concerns itself with Functional through to Infrastructure, Technology Architecture, Software through to Infrastructure, and Enterprise Architecture focuses strategically on the whole stack.  I've gone the long way around this (explanation), but we needed to be on the same page so that it is clear what is changing.  To a large degree layers 4 to 8 are managed and operated by SAP and its Partners within the various RISE models, alleviating the load on your organisations; operations, development and security teams.  Meaning:

  • Technology Architecture is now concerned primarily with Integration and Data Governance
  • IT Architecture is concerned with Functional requirements, and customisation governance
    • This is where most of the magic happens
  • Enterprise Architecture derives strategic objectives, mapping them to Business Capabilities and Processes whilst maintaining cohesion with existing policies and platforms.

On the other hand, your organizations Development, Security & Operations (DevSecOps) teams should be turning their attention to layers 1 through 5 (Business Capabilities to Software components)

  • Development - Less technical freedom with an increase in Extension Technology per Extension Task
  • Security - Reduced focus on technology, and an increase on compliance
  • Operations - Reduced focus on technology and an increase on monitoring and service management

It starts to become clear at this point why SAP has gone to the efforts they have, to reduce the skill entry threshold for development(extension), integration and data and analytics, as this is the where the IT Operating Model really starts to contribute value.  To help with this challenge, SAP has provided methodologies for each of the following domains:

  • Data and Analytics
  • Integration
  • Extension

And the SAP Trust Centre https://www.sap.com/australia/about/trust-center.html is now the primary online resource for your security and compliance teams in the shared responsibility model. 

These methodologies are not a 'paint with numbers' on how to how to create analytics dashboards, or extending business processes, you can find tutorials, missions, and reference architectures on https://developers.sap.com/ and https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/ which also includes, service and AI estimates and catalogues.  These methodologies are phased approaches, with ease entry characteristics, meaning your current IT Architecture maturity need not be barrier, in fact it can be an asset.  Each of the methodologies comprises of phases and steps, to facilitate organisations to leverage their current architectural assets that can be reverse engineered (backwards) or engineered (forwards) to gradually introduce stronger governance and reducing complexity.

For reference some of the SAP components are described below, with some links for further reading:

SAP Business Technology Platform

Simplistically the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) is a global platform of platforms (PaaS), and software solutions (SaaS), enabling (side-by-side) custom development, persistence (storage), automation, and AI capabilities, that can be leveraged and integrated with other SAP Software, Cloud and On-Premise.

A little more detail.  The SAP BTP also benefits from open standards to facilitate integration to non SAP software using pre-built, and features-rich connectors (80+ standard adapters, 170+ non-SAP connectors), de-risking integration efforts, reducing complexity and cost.  All underpinned by a foundational global, regional and subaccount model with a clear separation of concerns, such as data, identity and authentication.

More still.  There are three environments (Platform as a Service (PaaS)) available at the SAP BTP subaccount level; Kyma, ABAP, and Cloud Foundry, this is where side-by-side extensibility is achieved, with each having its own user and authorisation environment management.  This may sound onerous, however once you unpack the use cases, not only does it make sense, it gives your organisation the ability to choose the most appropriate Extension Technology per Task.  In my view this is 'cost optimised flexibility', if you recall my earlier statement in the section Mapping EA to ITOM above, 'ITOMs are commonly viewed as pyramids or as opposing forces of cost, quality and flexibility (but you can only really favour two)', your organisation can increase flexibility with SAP BTP as it is builtin, without significantly impacting cost, or quality.

HINT - Project managers you can add this last point to your list of intangible benefits.

GROWTH - Solution Architects learn about the various Enterprise Agreements, Environments and Runtimes at

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/3019

SAP Data and Analytics

Engulfed by the recent announcement of SAP Business Data Cloud SAP there are three product capabilities layers targeted at 4 users types:

  • Analytics and Planning
    • Application Users
  • Business Data Fabric
    • Analysts and Data modeller
  • Database layer
    • Administrators/developers

The traditional administrator role within operations needs to refocuses their efforts on capacity planning (storage), the ingress of structured, semi-structured and unstructured data, and facilitating security and privacy.

GROWTH - Administrators growth opportunity, learn SAP Datasphere security and capacity planning.  https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4104

Data modellers and analysts are primarily focused on the capabilities of SAP Datasphere and this naturally lands within the Data Fabric layer along with Master Data Management.  The aim of SAP Datasphere is to simplify your data landscape, whilst enriching data so that value can be extracted, this is productising data, and adding it to your Datasphere Catalogue.

COLLABORATE - Data modellers and analysts will be required to work closely with administrators to organise and control the level of visibility and collaboration of SAP Datasphere Spaces.

Application Users, this is where we see some of the specialist skills, traditionally the realm of Analysts and Data Modellers start to transition to 'Key Users' (formerly 'Citizen Developers'), with the help of generative AI to discover hidden insights, automate reporting and develop business plans with Joule Copilot.  Application users work mainly in SAP Analytics Cloud using simple UI elements to create 'Stories' and 'Analytic Applications' (lowered skill threshold).  SAP Analytics Cloud is a native SAP BTP SaaS product.

GROWTH - Application Users (Key Users) growth opportunity to learn the many SAP Analytics Cloud planning tools.  https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/3091/

SAP Integration Suite

The SAP Integration Suite is a set of capabilities rather than products, which collectively enable organisations to; Manage APIs, and business events, extend non-SAP connectivity, and more…  These capabilities are:

  • Cloud Integration
  • API ManagementGraph
  • Open Connectors
  • Integration Advisor
  • Trading Partner Management
  • Integration Assessment
  • Migration Assessment
  • SAP Event Mesh

The idea of an Application Factory, and Centre of Excellence comes from the SAPs Integration Strategy which suggests organisations maintain an 'Application Factory' paired with an 'Integration Centre of Excellence' to drive the following key imperatives:

  • A Platform Approach.
  • Agility and Scalability.
  • Integration Democratization.
  • Smart Lifecycle Management.
  • Analytics and Predictive Intelligence.

These 5 points are a little abstract, they are introduced here as key concepts, not just for integration, but in terms of Cross Platform Architecture and the Next Generation IT Operations Model to drive value of data products, analytics; stories and dashboards, and business process extensions by levelling up governance with a platform style approach.

The SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology contains a list of common integration roles, which are geared toward large enterprises.  My view is that the Factory and CoE need not be focused solely only on integration, and as such the role description can drop the title of integration:

  • Chief Integration Officer = Chief Information Officer
  • Integration Administrator = Administrator
  • Integration Developer = Developer
  • Integration Architect = Solution Architect
  • Enterprise Architect
  • Application/API Developer = Developer

GROWTH - Administrators, developers and architects should familiarise themselves with the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology Overview | SAP Help Portal along with the 100+ missions in the SAP Discovery Center Search - integrate

GROWTH - Executives can gain an understanding of SAP Integration Strategy in the White Paper Integrate to Innovate

With business and user roles of:

  • Business Domain Expert
  • Business User
  • Citizen Integrator = Key User

You could argue that some of these roles need to be integration specific, and that I have either devalued, or underplayed the skills required, and of course you'd be right depending on your organisational context.  But looking through a lens of industrialisation, do you need an integration developer 100% of the time?  I'd argue that most organisations need a developer who can scope integration domains, styles and patterns, in line with the well documented SAP methodology, and an integration developer could be augmented when required to create new integration assets for the Catalogue of Integration Use Patterns.

COLLABORATE - Solution Architects will need to work between your organisations Application Factory and Centre of Excellence to ensure current and evolving business requirements are serviceable by your organisations IT capabilities.

entry.title: A Weekend to Remember: SAP Inside Track Recife 2025
entry.content:
This weekend, I had the honor of participating in SAP Inside Track Recife, and I can confidently say—it was one of the best technology events hosted in the city over the past decade. As someone passionate about SAP and innovation, seeing the energy, knowledge sharing, and community spirit come together powerfully was truly inspiring.

Recife: Where Innovation Meets Culture

For those who haven’t visited, Recife is more than beautiful beaches, rich culture, and historic architecture. It’s also a thriving innovation center, home to the Porto Digital—the Polo Tecnológico de Recife. This technology hub gathers major consulting firms and companies that lead SAP implementations across Brazil. It's no coincidence that SIT Recife has grown to become such an impactful event—it sits right at the intersection of tech talent and business transformation.

What Made SIT Recife 2025 So Special?

  • High-level content delivered by top SAP experts and thought leaders

  • An engaged audience ready to share, question, and collaborate

  • A celebration of our growing SAP community in the Northeast

  • 🔹 And of course—great music, networking, and culture to close the day!

Agenda Highlights

Here are some of the insightful sessions that made this year’s event truly exceptional:

  • Marssel Vilaça – Case Study with Conversion Paths to S/4HANA

  • Fabio Dezan – Myths and Truths about the New Brazil Localization and the End of TAXBRA in S/4HANA Public Edition

  • Lucas Ferreira – How to Get the Most Out of FIORI with Customizations and Clean Core

  • Gabriel Magalhães – The End of SE38? The Future of ABAP Development in S/4HANA

  • Luciano Casado – Using Joule in S/4HANA Public Cloud

  • Antonio Figueredo – System Developed from BTP for Customer Relations

  • Manuel Robalinho & Rodrigo Albuquerque – Innovation in Banking Communication with SAP  (Case MAGNUM Tires)

  • Sérgio Ferreira – Exploring the Future with SAP Analytics and Business Data Cloud

  • Edson Inowe – AI in the SAP Ecosystem: Innovation, Efficiency, and the Future of Business Management

And to close the event? A traditional Forró music celebration and a prize draw that brought smiles all around. 

Thank You, Recife!

Huge thanks to the organizers, speakers, volunteers, and everyone who attended. Your energy made this an unforgettable experience.

This SIT Recife proved once again that when community and innovation meet, we go further—together.

See you next time, Recife!  
#SAPInsideTrack #SITRecife #SAPCommunity #SAPBrazil

entry.content:
ご担当者　様

平素よりお世話になっております。

SAP Solution Manager（SolMan）が停止した場合の影響は、SolManが提供する機能をどのようにご利用されているかに大きく依存します。

SolManでご利用中の主な機能は何でしょうか？

以下に一般的な影響範囲をまとめさせていただきます。

  • システム監視・アラート通知の停止
  • ITサービス管理（ITSM）・インシデント管理の停止
  • ChaRM（Change Request Management）の停止
  • ソリューションドキュメントやプロジェクト管理機能の利用不可
  • テスト管理（Test Suite）の停止
  • システムランドスケープの同期停止

ご参照お願い致します。

SAPサポート

entry.title: Re: Can we add any customized movement against production...
entry.content:
Normally, we can use the planned goods issue and unplanned goods issue to distinguish the case you mentioned. 

Planned and unplanned goods issues are two different types of material withdrawals in SAP:

  1. Planned Goods Issue:

    • References a reservation or production order.
    • System automatically creates a reservation for components planned in the order 
    • When posting the goods issue, you can reference the order or reservation 
    • The system determines all components to be withdrawn based on the reservation

  2. Unplanned Goods Issue:

    • Used when additional material or quantity is required during production that was not originally planned.
    • No existing reservation to reference 
    • Posted as a goods issue without reference
    • Can be used for various reasons like:
      • Withdrawal for cost center or project
      • Sample taking for quality inspection
      • Scrapping material
      • Unexpected material provision for production
    •  
    •  
    • In material document checking you can find the difference. 

      •

Hope this further clarifies this.

entry.title: Regarding Fixed Asset Depreciation Areas
entry.content:
Hi Dear experts,

Regarding Fixed Assets - Standard Depreciation Areas: I have 4 Questions.

① Is my understanding of the following depreciation areas correct?
　01: Book Depreciation ⇒ Accounting calculation
　15: Local Tax ⇒ Local corporate tax amount
　91: Corporate Tax (No Accelerated Depreciation) ⇒ Corporate tax amount
　92: Local GAAP vs. Tax Differences ⇒ (Book Depreciation - Local Tax) value = Indicates discrepancies between accounting and tax rules
　32: IFRS ⇒ IFRS-based calculation

② Is 90: Fixed Asset Tax equivalent to 15: Local Tax?

③ The asset values and depreciation amounts reported in the Depreciable Asset Declaration Form (償却資産申告書) and Annexed Table 16 (別表16) are based on the values from 01: Book Depreciation. Is this understanding accurate?

④ For depreciation areas related to tax calculations (e.g., 15), what are these values used for?

Please suggest.

Thank you very much.Best Regards!

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Asset Management SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance

entry.title: Re: 🚀 Share Your Best Prompts and Knowledge to Boost Productivity in the SAP Community! 🚀
entry.content:
Prompt for SAP Build Code - CAP Application:

You are a senior enterprise software architect specializing in SAP CAP (Cloud Application Programming) development. Your task is to generate a comprehensive prompt for creating a custom loyalty application using CDS (Core Data Services) modeling.

Input Variables:
1. Business Requirements:
<business_requirements>

</business_requirements>

2. Technical Constraints:
<technical_constraints>

</technical_constraints>

Prompt Generation Instructions:

1. Analyze Business Requirements:
- Carefully review the provided business requirements
- Identify key functional and non-functional requirements for the loyalty app
- Extract specific features, user roles, and business logic needs

2. Technical Design Considerations:
- Review technical constraints and platform limitations
- Determine appropriate SAP CAP and CDS modeling approach
- Consider integration points, data models, and service definitions

3. CDS Model Structure:
Generate a comprehensive CDS model that includes:
- Entities: Customer, LoyaltyPoints, Transactions, Rewards
- Relationships between entities
- Annotations for validation, authorization, and UI hints
- Calculated fields for point accumulation and reward eligibility

4. Service Definition:
Create a service definition that includes:
- CRUD operations for loyalty-related entities
- Custom actions for point redemption, reward claiming
- Event-driven capabilities for tracking customer interactions
- Role-based access control definitions

5. Application Logic Requirements:
Implement business logic for:
- Point calculation algorithms
- Reward tier management
- Transaction validation
- Personalized recommendation engine

6. Output Specification:
Provide a detailed prompt that includes:
- Comprehensive CDS model definition
- Service interface specifications
- Pseudo-code for key business logic
- Recommended implementation strategies
- Potential extension points

Prompt Generation Guidelines:
- Use clear, concise language
- Provide concrete examples where possible
- Highlight best practices for SAP CAP development
- Ensure scalability and performance considerations

Constraints and Validation:
- Ensure compliance with provided technical constraints
- Validate that all business requirements are addressed
- Recommend potential architectural patterns

Deliverable Format:
Generate the prompt as a structured markdown document with clear sections, code snippets, and explanatory comments.

Special Instructions:
- Prioritize clean, modular design
- Consider future extensibility
- Implement robust error handling
- Follow SAP CAP and CDS development best practices

entry.title: Developer Hub Now Displays Swagger Security Definitions
entry.content:
SAP API Management is a comprehensive solution that facilitates the effective management and integration of APIs across various systems and applications. One of the most valuable assets within SAP API Management is the Developer Hub, a centralized catalog of APIs and Events that has previously been known as API Business Hub Enterprise. Developer Hub serves as a marketplace where organizations can publish their APIs, making them easily discoverable and accessible to developers worldwide. Today, we are excited to announce a significant enhancement that further bolsters the Developer Hub’s capabilities: the rendering of security definitions in the Swagger renderer of the API references.

 

What is Developer Hub?

For those unfamiliar, Developer Hub is a one-stop catalog offering from SAP API Management, where application developers can discover, search for, and consume APIs published by the organization.It provides a comprehensive list of APIs and Events, along with detailed documentation and code examples, enabling developers to seamlessly integrate different systems and applications.

 

Introducing Security Definitions in Swagger

Swagger, now known as the OpenAPI Specification, is a broadly adopted standard for defining and documenting RESTful APIs. It not only simplifies API documentation but also makes it easier for developers to understand and integrate APIs into their applications. With the latest enhancement, the Developer Hub now renders security definitions directly within the Swagger renderer of the API references.

 

 

Why This Enhancement Matters?

1. Enhanced Security Transparency

One of the primary benefits of rendering security definitions within the Swagger renderer is the increased transparency it provides. Developers can now easily view the security requirements and authentication mechanisms directly within the API documentation. This clarity reduces the risk of misunderstandings and ensures that developers are aware of the security protocols they need to comply with.

2. Streamlined Development Process

By integrating security details into the Swagger renderer, developers no longer need to toggle between different documentation resources to understand the security requirements. Everything they need is consolidated in one place, streamlining the development process and saving valuable time.

3. Easy Generation of OAuth Tokens

The enhanced Swagger renderer in the Developer Hub now supports the easy generation of OAuth tokens without the need to use other applications like Postman. This simplifies the authentication process and enables developers to quickly test and validate API calls, thereby speeding up the development cycle.

 

Current Limitations

It is important to note that the rendering of security definitions in the Swagger renderer in Developer Hub is currently supported only at the root level and not at the individual resource levels. While this limitation exists, we are actively working to expand this functionality to cover resource-level security definitions in future updates.

 

Conclusion

The enhancement of rendering security definitions in the Swagger renderer is a testament to SAP’s commitment to continuously improving the Developer Hub and the overall integration experience. By making security information more accessible and transparent, SAP not only simplifies the development process but also ensures that integrated solutions are secure and compliant with best practices.

 

Stay tuned for more updates and enhancements as we continue to evolve the SAP API Management to better serve the needs of developers and enterprises alike. Happy integrating!

entry.title: Re: How to delete null entries/values during calculation...
entry.content:
Hi @amansingh006 

I am not clear on what deleting the whole row means, but if there are three measures, you can also delete the third measure.  

IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "thirdmeasure") = 0 OR RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "thirdmeasure") = null THEN
	DELETE([d/Measures]="FirstMeasure")
	DELETE([d/Measures]="SecondMeasure")
	DELETE([d/Measures]="thirdmeasure")
ENDIF

@N1kh1l 

DELETE() can delete the record itself. 

IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "thirdmeasure") = 0 OR RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "thirdmeasure") = null THEN
	DELETE([d/Measures]="thirdmeasure")
ENDIF

Regards,

Moonjun

entry.title: The APIVERSE – Developer Hub Now Renders Swagger Security!
entry.content: The OpenAPI Specification has become the go-to way to describe and document RESTful APIs. It eliminates the guesswork in API integration, making life a lot easier for developers. With the latest upgrade, Developer Hub in SAP Integration Suite now displays security definitions directly within the Swagger view—no more searching, no hassle—just everything you need in one place, making it much easier for developers to work with APIs. For more insights, check out the blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/developer-hub-now-displays-swagger-security-definitions/ba-p/14098607

entry.title: Re: How to get a negative value in selection criteria of...
entry.content:
Hi Joseph,

in my opinion you have 2 ways.

First
When using a date field you are able to insert -10 and the date will already be calculated with the right date, when pressing tab. You need to train your users to do so for your query.

Second
Use a quantity field, here it is possible to insert a negativ value (or find sonething similar)

/*Buchungsdatum*/
/**SELECT FROM [OFPR] T0 **/
DECLARE @DocDateFrom AS Date
/* WHERE */
SET @DocDateFrom = /* T0.F_RefDate */ '[%0]'

/**SELECT FROM [OFPR] T1 **/
DECLARE @DocDateTo AS Date
/* WHERE */
SET @DocDateTo = /* T1.T_RefDate */ '[%1]'

regards Lothar

entry.title: Re: Detecting Handwritten vs. Digital Docs in SAP CAP + DOX
entry.content:
I don't think we expose a flag "handwritten" or "printed" at the moment. We can consider this feature in the future if there is more demand. In the meantime, you can explore using a vision LLM model (eg GPT4o, Gemini 2.0, Claude 3.7) from Generative AI Hub together with a custom prompt (eg "You are a helpful assistant for documents. Determine if the attached document contains handwritten text or only consists of printed text. Only return the word 'handwritten' or 'printed'. Do not return anything else")

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/solving-your-business-problems-using-prompts-and-llms-in-sap-s-generative-ai-hub/exploring-generative-ai-hub-in-sap-ai-core

entry.title: PLM Tech Uncovered: SAP Integrated Product Development Update 04/2025
entry.content:
Welcome to the latest edition of this blog series, bringing you the most recent updates on SAP Integrated Product Development!

April 2025 brings the following highlights for your business:

Collaboration
Editing of configured custom fields in the collaboration header
In the My Collaborations app, when creating a collaboration, collaboration administrators can select or enter values to set up custom fields for collaboration headers. During editing, they can also modify or select these values. Custom collaboration header fields display according to the collaboration type configuration. These values appear in the header section of the collaboration.

Formulation
Ability to reflect evaporation and absorption in formulas
Formulas can now account for evaporation and absorption processes during production using a balancing item, which indicates the amount of water or another substance that is evaporated (negative value) or absorbed (positive value).

Specification Management
Specifications without deletable status are protected
You can configure statuses in Status Management with or without the deletable action. If a status without the deletable action is assigned to a specification, you can't use the delete button to delete that specification in the Manage Specifications app. This helps prevent the deletion of important specifications, such as released ones.

To stay informed until the next update you can:
➡️ Subscribe to the What’s New Viewer on SAP Integrated Product Development Help Portal 
🤝 Join our LinkedIn group SAP Solutions for PLM 
🌐 Visit us at www.sap.com/IPD

entry.title: Re: Test plan edit error "A row already exists with this...
entry.content: Maybe you can try the following solution of SAPnote:2621363 - Repair program for duplicate test package nodes. - Backup your system. - Run the program SMT_TPCK_NODE_DUPLICATE_DEL_1 first in test than in update mode. If the issue cannot be fixed, you can raise an incident with the issue details under the component: SV-SMG-TWB.

entry.title: TM planning group on FU stage level / propagation from root to stage
entry.content:
Dear TM experts,

I have a shipper scenario in TM Basic with inbound and outbound freight units. Therefore I want to select my freight units on planning group instead of source or destination location in the TM cockpit.

I determine planning group and purchasing organisation with a condition and assign it to the FU. I found out that the determined data (i.e. planning group) is only added to the FU root and not propagated to the FU stages. As a result, my TC selection does not find any of my FUs.

SAP note confirms this behavior: 

3059208 - Planning and Execution Organization is not propagated from the header / root level of the FU to its stage level - SAP for Me

Is there a way to propagate the FU root data 1:1 to the stages w/o extension?

Thank you.

entry.title: Business Rules in SAP SuccessFactors: Best Practices for High-Impact Delivery
entry.content:
Mastering SAP SuccessFactors Business Rules: From First Rule to Expert Tactics

Whether you're just starting out or looking to sharpen your edge as a SuccessFactors consultant, this guide combines foundational understanding with professional insights to help you master business rules in SAP SuccessFactors.

What Are Business Rules?

Let me keep it simple, business rules are SAP's way of letting the system automate decisions without writing code. They're logical statements that run when something happens — like when a user edits a field or saves a form.

  • Validating input (e.g., age on hire date)
  • Auto-filling values (e.g., pay grade based on department)
  • Triggering workflows
  • Controlling UI behavior (e.g., setting field visibility)
  • Example: If an employee is under 18, show an error.
  • Example: If department = Sales, then auto-set Pay Grade = S05.

Where Are Business Rules Used?

  • Employee Central: Validate data, default fields, enforce policies
  • Recruiting: Auto-screen applications, assign hiring managers
  • Onboarding: Trigger compliance tasks and so on....

Key Concepts

Term & What it means

Base ObjectThe object your rule logic is tied to (e.g., Job Info)
TriggerEvent that runs the rule (onChange, onSave, onInit)
ScenarioThe predefined rule category used at creation
ConditionThe "if" portion of the rule
ActionThe "then" result of the condition being true

Step-by-Step Rule Planning Guide

  1. Understand the requirement – What is the business asking for?
  2. Identify the data fields – Which fields are involved?
  3. Choose the base object – What section of data is being affected?
  4. Select the trigger event – When should the rule run?
  5. Pick the correct rule scenario – See below!
  6. Draft the logic in plain language – If A, then B
  7. Build, assign, and test – Deploy and validate behavior

Prerequisites Before Writing Business Rules

  • Role-Based Permissions: Ensure you have access to Configure Business Rules, Manage Business Configuration, and relevant HRIS elements.
  • HRIS Elements and Fields Exist: Your fields must exist in BCUI or XML before referencing them in a rule.
  • Synced Data Model: If using XML, make sure data model is up-to-date and properly imported.
  • Correct Rule Scenarios: SAP has deprecated “Basic” — use specific application scenarios (e.g., Job Info – Validate onSave).
  • Lookup, Workflow, and Messages: If your rule depends on these, ensure they're created first.
  • Use Internal Field IDs: Always reference fields by their technical name (e.g., jobInfo.startDate).

Choosing the Right Base Object

  • Use the object that holds your target fields
  • Use Model objects to compare old/new values or control UI
  • Use Employee Information Model (hire screen) to access multiple portlets in one rule

How to Pick the Right Rule Type

Use Case & Recommended Rule Type

Stop incorrect inputValidation Rule 
Auto-fill valuesDerivation Rule
Start workflowWorkflow Rule
Set field visibility/requirementUI Attribute (Model-based)

Selecting the Right Scenario

Note: The "Basic" scenario is deprecated. Always choose a predefined scenario like:

  • Trigger onPostSave Events for Job Information
  • Trigger Workflows
  • Configure Onboarding or Offboarding Process Flow

Use SAP’s official rule scenario guide: View on SAP Help Portal

 

Examples:

Validate Hire Age

IF Difference In Calendar Years(personInfo.dateOfBirth, jobInfo.startDate) < 18
THEN Raise Error: "Hire date must be after 18th birthday"

Auto-Assign Pay Grade by Department

IF jobInfo.department == "Sales"
THEN set jobInfo.payGrade = "S05"
ELSE IF jobInfo.department == "Engineering"
THEN set jobInfo.payGrade = "E05"

Workflow for International Hires

IF jobInfo.country != "US"
THEN set changeWorkflow = "INT_ONBOARD_WF"

Testing Tips

  • Test different users and data scenarios
  • Use real HRIS portlets and test onChange and onSave separately
  • Leverage the Admin Center Check Tool to catch errors

Pro Tips

  • Use consistent naming conventions (e.g., Validate_Hire_Age)
  • Comment rules with a business reason
  • Test thoroughly in preview before going live
  • Break large rules into smaller reusable ones
  • Use lookup tables instead of hardcoded values

Final Thoughts

Business rules are your key to making SAP SuccessFactors work for real business needs, not just system configuration. Whether you're new to the platform or streamlining a global rollout, rules that are clear, tested, and purpose-driven will keep your clients happy and your projects clean.

What was your first business rule? Or your favorite trick? Share it in the comments!

Stay tuned for upcoming blog posts where I’ll dive deeper into real-world use cases. I’ll be talking thru over 50+ ready-to-use SAP SuccessFactors business rules. These are perfect for internal admins and consultants alike who are working on live projects and want proven, time-saving solutions.

entry.title: Crystal Reports Viewer SP36 - Load report failed in SAP Crystal Reports
entry.content:
Hi,

I am getting below error on our prod servers when users try to generate crystal reports.

Load report failed. at CrystalDecisions.ReportAppServer.ReportClientDocumentWrapper.EnsureDocumentIsOpened() at CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine.ReportDocument.Load(String filename, OpenReportMethod openMethod, Int16 parentJob) at CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine.ReportDocument.Load(String filename)

The crystal report runtimes which are installed on server are below. Apart from below we do not have any other crystal related software installed on server -

  • SAP Crystal Reports runtime engine for .NET Framework (32 bit) - 13.0.36.5040
  • SAP Crystal Reports runtime engine for .NET Framework (64 bit) - 13.0.36.5040

We have below configuration on our servers -

  • OS - Windows Server 2022
  • IIS 10
  • RAM - 16 GB

Some key notes-

The "Load report failed" issue is intermittent. When the issue comes for any report , our support team recycles the IIS app pool and the error usually not comes for some time (but it appears again after some days/ hours).

The issue comes up on many of our servers, but it is quite frequent for those of our clients who have huge volume data in reports. We have some other downloadable CSV reports apart from crystal reports.

We have a good and ample disk size on each of our servers.

Workaround tried so far -

PrintJobLimit - Currently it is set to 75 (default) on our servers. We tried modifying this to 500 and even -1 but this not resolves the issue.

Temp folder permissions - Permissions seems ok on our servers. Also the issue is intermittent so the reason of permission issue can be ruled out.

Report Code Cleanup / Object Disposition - We are using below code lines in our Page_Unload event for report object disposition -

Reportdocument.Close()

Reportdocument.Dispose()

Please advise on what I should do to permanently remove this load report failed error for my clients.

Thanks in advance.

 

SAP Crystal Reports viewer

entry.title: Re: Managed aspects in SAP CAPM stores the SCIM id of SAP...
entry.content: This is configured in the trust configuration for your app (BTP Cloud Foundry org) in your IAS tenant. Select the correct Subject Name Identifier which is sent from IAS to BTP. See here: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/configure-subject-name-identifier-sent-to-application

entry.title: SAC - Week number calculation depending from "date input control" and "pc system date"
entry.content:
Hello everyone

I really need your help to calculate the number of weeks, between an "date input control" and the pc system date (of course this value should be recalculated every time I change the date in the "date input control").

Then I need to use this value (named as example WeekNum) to calculate the average of the turnover, that is AvgTurnOver = Turnover / WeekNum

So I have an input date control (upper left side of my printscreen), in this case Apr 2025, and I want to calculate a new measure that depends from this date in weeks since 01 Jan of the year of input date control.

 

 

In this case, if I count the weeks between 01/01/2025 and 30/04/2025 they are 17 and then I should use this value to calculate the average.

Suppose I change the date input control to Nov 2024, I need to have as result the number of weeks between 01/01/2024 and 30/11/2024.

How can I achieve this in a formula calculation?

Thank you for your support , I really appreciate your suggest

Adriano
